threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You stand baffled at the bus stop, staring at the timetable like it’s a puzzle, while everyone else knows exactly when the next one will show up.
Saw a guy on the 381 last night arguing with his reflection in the window. Kept swearing at it like it was an ex. Only in Bermondsey.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a couple arguing loudly in Turkish, switch to perfect English mid-fight, then share a smug smile. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those old U-Bahn stations? Some still have their original tiles from the 1920s. They feel like stepping into a time capsule, especially at nights.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're local when they can name the taco truck on every corner but can't tell you who runs BART. It's all about priorities.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy practicing his stand-up on the 19 Polk today. Only in the Tenderloin would folks be cracking up on a crowded bus at 8 AM.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the locals flock to Park Güell. Most of us avoid it like the plague—better off at a quiet bar on Verdi.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new when they still look puzzled at the 'bocadillos' pricing at local shops, trying to figure out how a sandwich costs more than a pint.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to stand on the right on the escalators. Hope you enjoy your morning workout dodging the locals!
Last night on the 77, a saxophonist jumped on at Brixton and played until he got off at Stockwell. That's the kind of late-night magic you don't get on the tube.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a guy juggling flaming torches at Hermannplatz while his dog waited patiently next to a kebab shop.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you grab a currywurst at 4 AM after a night out, while street artists create spontaneous graffiti performances on the sidewalks.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can always tell who's local by whether they know the secret entrance to Dolores Park. Tourists just get lost at the main gate.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo serenade a lady with a ukulele on the 14-Mission. Only in SF do you catch a romantic moment like that on your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think Gràcia is just Gaudí, but the real charm is in the little plazas and the local bakery smell that hits you at dawn.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell the newcomers by their confusion at the lack of signs for 'caution' at crossings—watching them hesitate at every corner is like a comedy show.
